change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value:
Operations and office set up, including procurement and recruitment, selection of partners and signing of agreements.
	
		Capacity building of staff and partners on UNSCR 1325&1820, CEDAW, gender and social inclusion and policies and legislation on sexual explotation and abuse.
	
		Development of M&E plan (in cooperation with CARE Österreich),  including plan and process for project review
	
		Development of exit strategy in consultation with implementing partners.
	
		Conduct base line survey on knowledge, attitude and practice (KAP)
	
		Conduct analysis of poverty, power structures and gender analysis through assessment of Underlying Causes of Poverty, community mapping and other participatory assessment tools.
	
		Strengthen women’s community based organisations and Popular Education Centres, Local Peace Committees through capacity building.
	
		Awareness generation among VDC and DDCs,  political parties and other line agency on UNSCR 1325 and 1820, women’s rights, gender and social inclusion (training, interactions, workshop, debate and distribution of resource materials etc)
	
		Mobilisation of community based organisations of women (and others) to provide critical feedback and inputs to drafts of the constitution.
	
		Strenghtening women’s networks (N-FOWRC) from district to national levels by organizing capacity building training, interactions and providing support to organize campagins, rallies and public hearing  to end impunity and to promote women’s advancement.
	
		Develop and conduct psycho-education sessions on reactions to distress, first interventions for communities.
	
		Assessment of local/ traditional psychosocial coping mechanisms in Kapilvastu and assessment of harmful traditions.
	
		Trained local volunteers will conduct various psychosocial activities (e.g. facilitating group discussion among women facing distress to provide forum for sharing, ventilation,strengthening life skills to get affirmation and for raising awareness among these women about their rights and how they can challenge oppressive practices.)
	
		Community based support by  thorough information for women in need on institutions offering mental health, social, medical or other specialized services on basis of the assessment of services. (Provision of emergency fund to support women survivors of violence to meet their costs for medical care, tranportation, food  and shelter.)
	
		Capacity building of community based psycho social care providers, traditional healers and local health workers. Organize psychosocial care worker’s capacity building activities and provide support  to initiate interventations for wellbeing through group facilitation and mobilization
	
		Support to create a loose forum of service providers (police, health service poviders, public prosecuter, administers and concerned groups such as KOSHISH group) to share information and learnings inoder to render gender responsive service to the survivors) and creating community safety nets.
	
		Support to psychosocial care workers in terms of training in stress management, emotional support regular sharing meetings, caregiver peer support, etc.
	
		Assessment of existing services available for psychosocial and mental health care, health care, legal aid and identify gaps and advocate for addressing gaps.
	
		Facilitate linkages and coordination with professional mental health care providers, psychosocial counsellors, and other referral institutions (legal aid cells) to provide specific services to women survivors of violence (at local and national levels).
	
		Conduct workshops with influential members of community (schoolteachers, religious leaders, ward members, youth and elected women members, traditional healers), on consequences of harmful traditional practices (e.g. child marriage, etc) and provide support for regular interaction to monitor the progress.
	
		Train men as trainers on masculinities, gender based violence and social inclusion. to promote critical thinking around masculinities, gender norms and GBV and accountability
	
		Organise districts level workshops for reflection on maculinities, role of men and boys to end  GBV, women empowerment  and gender equality.
	
		Sensitisation programmes among communities (men and women) for critical thinking to promote behaviour change. Organize PEC centers at community levels for for critical thinking to promote behaviour change on harmful socio-cultural practices at household and community and awareness on UNSCR 1325/1820, gender issues, women’s rights, social exclusion and caste based discrimination.
	
		Mass awareness on UNSCR 1325/1820, gender issues, women’s rights, social exclusion (including caste based) through popular media such as radio and TV, theatre and wall newspaper.
	
		Support to demystify Constitution and legal provisions ( family law, citizeship and nationality laws and laws addressing GBV) by providing information in simple Nepali and loc

snapshot.executing_agency: Ministry of Women, Children and Senior Citizens, Ministry of Federal Affairs & General Administration

en.full: Women Empowerment for transformation in Churia Area Project (SAKCHHAM II)

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
